inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.handoffConfirm: Turn off AI for this conversation?
inbox.emptyBody: Conversations appear when Meta sends a webhook for a DM to your connected account. Send a test DM from another Instagram account to your business profile, then wait a few seconds. If nothing shows up, confirm in Meta: Webhooks callback URL, verify token, and subscription to Instagram messaging fields (see Settings → Integrations).
inbox.emptyRealtime: Tip: enable Supabase Realtime on tables conversations and messages so new DMs appear without refreshing the page.

integrations.oauthNativeBlurb: Opens Instagram's own OAuth (api.instagram.com). In the Meta app: Instagram product → API with Instagram login → add the exact redirect URL (same as under Facebook Login). Same App ID and App Secret.
integrations.oauthAccountPickerHint: If you use Facebook or Login for Business: pick your Facebook profile (Page admin) in Meta's switcher, not Instagram-only, or the flow may fail.
integrations.oauthBlurb: Login for Business (config ID) or classic scopes; links below.
integrations.errorInstagramNativeApp: Instagram Login isn't set up correctly in Meta (e.g. Invalid platform app). Check the Instagram product, OAuth redirect URIs, and use a professional Instagram account.
integrations.errorMissingBusinessConfig: Set META_BUSINESS_LOGIN_CONFIG_ID in Vercel: Meta App → Facebook Login for Business → Configurations → copy the configuration ID. Add your redirect URL there and under Facebook Login settings.
integrations.errorInvalidState: OAuth session expired. Try connecting again.
integrations.errorNoOrg: No organization found for your account.
integrations.errorNoInstagram: No Instagram Business account found on your Pages. Link IG to a Page in Meta Business Suite, then try again.
integrations.errorDenied: Meta login was cancelled or denied.
integrations.prodTokensNote: Production: store Meta tokens with Supabase Vault — never commit plaintext.
integrations.liveSetupWebhookLine: In Meta Developer: Webhooks → Callback URL: {url}
integrations.liveSetupVerifyLine: Webhook verify token must exactly match META_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN in your server env.
integrations.liveSetupSubscribeLine: Subscribe to messaging fields for Instagram. If your webhook shows object "page" (Facebook Page), apply the Supabase migration `facebook_page_id` and reconnect once with the Facebook OAuth link so we can match your Page ID.
integrations.liveSetupGeminiLine: Set GEMINI_API_KEY on the server (e.g. Vercel). The webhook calls the AI directly; without this key, messages are stored but not auto-replied.
integrations.liveSetupMissingAppUrl: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to your public site URL so we can show the exact webhook callback to paste in Meta.
integrations.webhookDiagHint: If the Inbox stays empty, run this and fix any item shown (especially SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY on Vercel).
integrations.webhookSimulateHint: Sends the same kind of POST Meta would send. If Inbox still stays empty after success, check Vercel Logs for [meta-webhook] POST received, then open Inbox.
integrations.webhookSimulateNoAccount: Connect an Instagram account with a page_id first.
integrations.webhookSimulateOk: OK — open Inbox or run diagnostics again (conversationCount should be ≥ 1).
integrations.webhookSimulateFail: Webhook returned {status}. Check Vercel deployment and env.

privacy.metaDescription: How we collect, use, and protect personal data when you use our Instagram and Facebook messaging automation service.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Body: This Privacy Policy describes how {appName} ("we", "us") processes personal data when you use our software service (the "Service") that helps businesses manage Instagram and Facebook direct messages, automation, and related sales workflows. {appName} is operated in connection with the website and application where this policy is published.
privacy.s2Body:
Account and identity: information you provide when you register or sign in (for example, email address and authentication data from your identity provider).

Service and integration data: when you connect Meta (Instagram/Facebook), we receive and store tokens and identifiers needed to operate messaging features, webhooks, and API calls on your behalf, as permitted by your connection and Meta's terms.

Messaging and CRM data: content and metadata from conversations, leads, campaigns, bookings, and settings you configure in the Service, to the extent needed to provide the product.

Technical data: logs, diagnostics, and security information (for example, IP address, device/browser type, timestamps) to run, secure, and improve the Service.
privacy.s3Body: We use personal data to provide and maintain the Service; authenticate users; process integrations with Meta and other third-party tools you enable; operate automation and AI-assisted features you configure; analyze usage in aggregate to improve reliability and performance; comply with law; and enforce our terms and protect users.
privacy.s4Body: Where enabled, the Service may use automated systems (including large language models) to generate suggested replies or classify conversations based on content you receive through connected channels. You remain responsible for your use of automated messaging and for complying with Meta's Platform Terms, advertising policies, and applicable laws (including consent and transparency requirements where they apply).
privacy.s5Body: When you connect Instagram or Facebook, your use of Meta products is also governed by Meta's terms and policies. We access and process data through Meta's APIs only as authorized by you and as permitted by Meta. We do not control Meta's own processing; please review Meta's privacy materials for how Meta handles data on its platforms.
privacy.s6Body: We use trusted infrastructure and service providers to host and operate the Service (for example, cloud hosting, database and authentication, email, analytics, and payment processing). Providers may process data in countries other than your own. We take steps designed to protect personal data through contractual and technical safeguards appropriate to the risk.
privacy.s7Body: We retain personal data for as long as needed to provide the Service, meet legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce agreements. We implement technical and organizational measures intended to protect data against unauthorized access, loss, or alteration. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure.
privacy.s8Body: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or export certain personal data, or to object to or restrict certain processing. To exercise rights or ask questions about this policy, contact us using the contact method shown in the Service or on our website. If we act as a processor on behalf of your organization, your administrator may be the right contact for some requests.

onboarding.connectMetaManualSuffix: You can also add a manual token under Settings → Integrations if needed.
